mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling kinases to reduce proliferative signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML; range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM (top 1–3 µM)

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3/RAF/VEGFR-family kinases to suppress oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Used off-label in FLT3-ITD AML; range given as 0.5 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases including FLT3
additional_notes: Range given as 1 nM–10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits multiple tyrosine kinases involved in growth/survival signaling
additional_notes: Text notes 'some AML activity reports'; range given as 3 nM–30 µM

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases, impacting AML signaling networks
additional_notes: Range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to reduce JAK-STAT pathway signaling driven by cytokines
additional_notes: Text notes combos often better; range given as 3 nM–10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/transcription and promote growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name in text: SAHA; approved in CTCL; tested in AML; range given as 10 nM–30 µM

mechanism: Pan-HDAC inhibition leading to epigenetic/transcriptional disruption and anti-proliferative effects
additional_notes: Approved in myeloma; described as potent; range given as 0.3 nM–1 µM

mechanism: Weak HDAC inhibition leading to epigenetic modulation
additional_notes: Repurposed in AML trials historically; note in text: high range due to low in vitro potency

mechanism: Incorporates into nucleic acids and inhibits DNMTs, reducing DNA methylation
additional_notes: Approved in AML/MDS; range given as 1 nM–10 µM; chemically unstable per notes (prepare fresh)

mechanism: Inhibits DNMTs after incorporation into DNA, causing hypomethylation and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Approved in AML; range given as 1 nM–10 µM; chemically unstable per notes (prepare fresh)

mechanism: Activates retinoic acid receptors to drive differentiation programs
additional_notes: Alternative names in text: ATRA, tretinoin; mostly APL but can affect subsets/combos; light/temperature sensitive

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 to promote mitochondrial apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in AML; text notes MOLM-13 sensitivity depends on conditions; range given as 0.01 nM–1 µM (often test up to 1 µM)

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-XL to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name in text: ABT-263; more toxic clinically but useful in vitro; range given as 0.1 nM–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in myeloma; text notes rarely need >100 nM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, inducing proteostasis stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Described as potent

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial respiratory complex I, altering cellular energy metabolism
additional_notes: Text notes variable AML effects; often needs mM

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial electron transport to impair bioenergetics
additional_notes: Text notes reported anti-leukemia activity

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase; cytotoxicity often copper-dependent
additional_notes: Practical note: consider testing ± CuCl2 (0.5–2 µM) condition

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing oxidative stress and promoting cell death
additional_notes: Text notes leukemia activity reported; range given as 1 nM–3 µM
